verdicts.note: Airtight: dossier documents no elected or governmental record of any kind, two prior single-digit primary finishes, and explicitly anti-establishment rhetoric ('Organize or die') [S1][S4]. Maximal outsider case; high confidence justified by the documented record.

Her entire platform is direct-democracy proceduralism (parallel public forums as 'a standing check against bad policy', pin-drop maps), stated repeatedly across pamphlet and website [S1][S2]; rubric pole_b explicitly names direct democracy. High confidence is warranted by repeated explicit statements.

Enacted record (JumpStart payroll tax, $200M+/yr) plus campaign materials touting it as a model for progressive revenue over cuts [S2][S7] supports +2 high. Separate flag on highlights: the bullet crediting JumpStart cites [S4], but the dossier sources JumpStart to [S2][S7] (S4 covers the rent-algorithm ban and procurement items), and the 'raised roughly ten times her nearest opponent' bullet cites [S1] where the dossier sources fundraising to [S5]. Highlight citation tags should be corrected.

+1 understates the evidence. Every documented budget posture is invest-and-expand: JumpStart revenue funding housing/climate/economic programs [S2][S7], doubled affordable-housing investments [S3], investments in transit, childcare, healthcare [S1], and a budget amendment backfilling federal Continuum of Care cuts [S4]. The dossier contains zero audit/restraint/oversight content for her. That is fully pole_b, symmetric with Duda's -2 for a fully oversight-first message.

Borderline check: her impact-fee legislation 'to reduce affordable-housing development barriers' [S4] has a supply-side flavor, but it is a minor item in service of affordable housing, against a dominant tenant-protection and public-investment record (rent-algorithm ban, doubled affordable-housing investment, supportive housing, anti-displacement) [S1][S2][S3][S4]. +2 high stands.

Repeated services-first framing (behavioral health, gun-violence prevention in pamphlet [S1]; connect trafficking victims to services in Times interview [S6]) with no enforcement planks documented. +1 rather than +2 is correctly conservative since she takes no explicit anti-enforcement positions.

Direction and medium confidence are right (SCAP co-sponsorship [S4], 'green housing development' [S2]), but the basis also leans on the Washington Conservation Action endorsement, which the dossier sources to the pamphlet [S1], not to the cited S2/S4. Add S1 or drop that clause from the basis.
A decade of elected record across two councils with specific passed legislation and regional board leadership [S3][S4][S6]. Maximal proven-record case; -2 high is airtight.

Checked for exaggeration: he is not anti-tax ('willing taxpayers' [S1]; taxes-funded programs should meet 'the highest standards' [S4]), but 'families squeezed by... taxes' plus skepticism of 'asking for more and delivering too little' [S1] supports a mild -1 at medium. Calibration is right.

Oversight is his core, repeated message across pamphlet and Times interview: independent auditing, outcomes-not-messaging, financial oversight of KCRHA and DCHS, Detroit as cautionary tale [S1][S2][S4]. Fully pole_a; -2 high justified.

-2 overstates it. His marquee proposals lean pole_a ('more aggressive law enforcement' on public drug use, 'three strikes' to mandatory treatment [S4]), but the pamphlet frames a 'more balanced approach' that 'prioritizes treatment, recovery, safer public spaces' [S1], and the dossier's own scoring note says he is enforcement-oriented 'while still framing treatment and recovery as goals.' A fully-pole_a -2 does not fit a candidate whose stated identity is balance; -1 does, especially with the enforcement specifics resting on a single source [S4].

+2 is reserved for the maximal outsider/renewal case, which in this race is Jacobson (no governmental experience at all). Duda is a first-time candidate but has 10 months as a Seattle council legislative aide who shepherded the library-levy legislation [S4], and he sought appointment to a council seat in January 2024 [S5] — an insider bid the score erases. The contest dossier itself distinguishes 'staff-level and private-sector experience' from 'no governmental experience.' +1 preserves that documented distinction.

missing.basis: Pole_a is 'coalition-building within existing party/donor structures gets results.' The dossier documents exactly that profile: 200+ consolidated institutional Democratic, labor, and advocacy endorsements [S1][S7] and a fundraising operation roughly ten times her nearest opponent's [S5], with no reform planks (PAC refusal, term limits, direct democracy) anywhere in her materials. Strong endorsement pattern supports medium per the guide's confidence tiers.
